member.name: Rishi Sunak

addresses: Unit 1, Omega Business Village, Northallerton, DL6 2NJ

member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Rishi Sunak is the Conservative MP for Richmond and Northallerton , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Catterick Garrison
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many (a) serving personnel and (b) dependents will be based at Catterick in each year between 2017 and 2020.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The planned estimated number of Service personnel to be based at Catterick between 2017 and 2020 is provided below: 2017201820192020Number of Service Personnel6,9606,9607,7007,890 Figures are rounded to the nearest ten and based upon internal Army planni...

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Vocational Guidance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what progress has been made on simplifying, clarifying and rationalising the quality awards system for careers education, information and guidance in England's schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recommends that all secondary schools should work towards a quality award for careers education, information, advice and guidance as an effective means of carrying out a self-review and external evaluation of the school’s careers programme....

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health, what steps his Department is taking to improve the safety of maternity care.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In November 2015, the Department announced an ambitious campaign to halve the national rates of stillbirths, neonatal and maternal deaths and brain injuries occurring during or soon after birth, by 2030.The Government has already invested in a capital fun...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Business: Government Assistance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask Mr Chancellor of the Exchequer, what fiscal steps he is taking to support businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government wants low taxes, but taxes that are paid. We are cutting corporation tax to 17%, the lowest rate in the G20, reducing the business rates burden in England by £6.7bn over 5 years, and acting on avoidance to ensure a level playing field for a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Care
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health, what progress the Government has made on integrating and improving care provided outside of hospitals.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through the £5.3 billion Better Care Fund local authorities and the National Health Service are using pooled budgets to deliver integrated care for their local populations. The NHS is working with 29 vanguard sites across the country as part of the New ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Economic Situation: North of England
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Communities and Local Government, what steps his Department is taking to support the economy in the North of England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is investing in science and technology, transport, digital and innovation, culture and tourism across the North, including in key infrastructure in my hon Friend’s constituency. Alongside the devolution of powers to Northern cities, towns a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
